facts: IIT Hyderabad (Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad) is located at Kandi, Sangareddy, Hyderabad, Telangana.
State language: Telugu. Campus languages: English, Hindi, Telugu, and Tamil.
Climate: A bit sunny, some days cloudy.
Sangareddy Bus Stop is the nearest bus stop to IIT Hyderabad.
Lingampalli Railway Station is the nearest railway station to IIT Hyderabad.
Rajiv Gandhi International Airport is the nearest airport to IIT Hyderabad.
IIT Hyderabad has 3 boys hostels for 1st year UG students, all with double sharing rooms.
Vivekananda Hostel (double sharing, 1st year boys).
SN Bose Hostel (double sharing, 1st year boys).
Ramanuja Hostel (double sharing, 1st year boys).
IIT Hyderabad has 1 girls hostel for 1st year UG students with double sharing rooms.
Kalpana Chawla Hostel (double sharing, 1st year girls).
IIT Hyderabad has 12 single sharing boys hostels for senior students.
Visweswaraya Hostel (single sharing).
Ramanujan Hostel (single sharing).
Sarabhai Hostel (single sharing).
Bhabha Hostel (single sharing).
Kalam Hostel (single sharing).
Susruta Hostel (single sharing).
Kautilya Hostel (single sharing).
Varahamira Hostel (single sharing).
Brahmagupta Hostel (single sharing).
Vyasa Hostel (single sharing).
Charaka Hostel (single sharing).
Raman Hostel (single sharing).
IIT Hyderabad has 6 single sharing girls hostels for senior students.
Aryabhatta Hostel (single sharing).
Gargi Hostel (single sharing).
Maitreyi Hostel (single sharing).
Anandhi Joshi Hostel (single sharing).
Sarojini Naidu Hostel (single sharing).
Bhaskara Hostel (single sharing).
Total: 22 hostels at IIT Hyderabad.
Boys: 15 hostels (3 double sharing for 1st years + 12 single sharing for seniors).
Girls: 7 hostels (1 double sharing for 1st years + 6 single sharing for seniors).
IIT Hyderabad has two messes: Old mess and New mess.
Veg menu is included in the base mess fee.
Veg and non-veg extras are available and paid separately.
Mess cost: Rs 130 per day.
Semester mess expense: Rs 15,470 (Rs 130 x 119 days).
Mid-semester break of 9 days is not counted (pay-per-meal during break).
LAN ports are available in hostel rooms at IIT Hyderabad.
Attendance depends on the courses taken. No fixed mandatory percentage.
Fests are held for almost every cultural occasion, including DJ nights.
Elan & nVision is the main annual fest of IIT Hyderabad.
Milan is the inter-hostel fest.
Diesta is the inter-department fest.
Washing machines are available in hostels.
Ironing facility is available in the old canteen area.
Volleyball, Tennis, Basketball, Badminton, Cricket, Athletics, Football, Chess, Carroms, and Foosball are available.
Old canteen at IIT Hyderabad.
New canteen with cafes and bakeries.
Mobile accessories shop in old canteen area.
Stationery shop in old canteen area.
Barber shop in old canteen area.
Two gyms: one in the sports complex and another nearby.
No trainer available.
No membership fee required (FREE for all students).
In-campus hospital at IIT Hyderabad.
Services are paid.
Medical Insurance Rs 1,888 per year with Rs 2 lakh coverage.
No curfew at IIT Hyderabad.
Students can enter and leave the campus at any time, on any day.
No fines for late entry or exit.
Every course has a designated time slot.
Students create their own timetable by selecting course slots.
State language: Telugu. On campus: English, Hindi, Telugu, and Tamil.
A bit sunny, some days cloudy at IIT Hyderabad, Kandi, Sangareddy.
